because.text: Re-ran Kimi on a tight pack (four Lean fronts + GOV only, single pass, hard emit-finished-doc instruction) after the first run exhausted its budget on a full firehose. Clean 351-word review, zero briefing artifacts. All five findings accepted. P1-1 fixed: proofs/README overstated WaveAlgebra TCB isolation - interfere_valid/cascade/crystallization transitively consume native_decide LUT lemmas, so the compiler is in their dependency cone; only the pure integer lemmas + left_dominance_ph are fully kernel-checked. TCB note rewritten. P2-1: tactic-transparency comment maps each fun_induction branch of step_bounds to grind's manual skeleton; both proofs still compile, eval bridge 33/33. P1-2/P1-3/P2-2 accepted and adjudicated P2 (clarity) not P1 (divergence): three GOV §3 prose gaps (freeze operational response, non-deriving-verifier key-state routing, zero-hop lineage base case) where the reference verifier already behaves as each clarification would specify - no conforming divergence today. Exact text prepared; held for a GOV 1.0.1 patch governed release since §3 is frozen at STANDARD and these are non-behavioral. Harness lesson: for a deep-but-narrow audit a small focused pack beats the full pack.

subject.note: Kimi k2.6 focused formal review (tight pack, single pass): clean, artifact-free; no P0; TCB precision + tactic note fixed, 3 GOV §3 clarity gaps held for a 1.0.1 patch
